acceptance.assertion: fleet/repos.yml remains the only normative Fleet membership source. A versioned Renovate rollout policy and deterministic scope projection are validated against active projectable Fleet repositories; archived-reference and fleet:false repositories are rejected and no independent complete Fleet list becomes authoritative.

Metarepo contains a versioned central Renovate V1 preset that disables automerge, bounds PR concurrency and cadence, groups compatible patch/minor updates, isolates major updates, and preserves existing GitHub/CI and Grabowski head/diff/review merge gates.

Validation fails closed when one repository/dependency scope would be assigned overlapping Renovate and Dependabot version-update producers. V1 keeps Dependabot Security Updates conceptually separate from version-update ownership and does not disable security alerts.

The policy encodes bounded waves for Mitschreiber custom-manager pilot, HausKI/HausKI-Audio volume pilot, RepoGround signal-quality pilot, Weltgewebe multi-ecosystem pilot, Metarepo control-plane cutover and later eligible Fleet repositories. Consumer-repository mutations remain separate follow-up tasks with their own claims, leases and rollback.

The rollout contract requires any Mend-hosted Renovate installation to be limited to explicitly selected repositories and requires drift comparison between expected Git-authored rollout scope and observed GitHub App repository scope. No all-repository installation is accepted in V1.

The 2026-06-23 through 2026-07-23 baseline of 51 Dependabot PRs, 40 merged and 11 closed-unmerged is stored as dated comparison evidence with per-repository counts where available, without asserting Renovate improvement before measurement.

The task records that repo.mitschreiber is currently absent from the Bureau repository-resource catalog and does not invent a claim. Required consumer-cutover and missing-resource follow-ups are registered through operator-native Bureau intake instead of being hidden in prose.

The metarepo foundation is independently reversible by reverting the policy/preset/tooling commit; no consumer Dependabot configuration, Renovate App permission, Bureau queue, claim, deployment or security-update state is changed by this task.

approval.note: The user explicitly instructed on 2026-07-23 to implement the newly reviewed system-wide Renovate plan. This task changes only metarepo policy/tooling and publishes no Renovate GitHub App installation or consumer-repository cutover.

goal: Supersede the T044 targeted-only Renovate boundary after the user's explicit 2026-07-23 decision by establishing the canonical Fleet-bound Renovate V1 policy, scope projection, duplicate-producer guard, baseline metrics and staged rollout contract in metarepo. This task does not yet activate Renovate in consumer repositories; it creates the reviewed foundation required for later repository-specific cutovers without introducing a second Fleet, lifecycle, merge, deployment or security truth.

does_not_establish: Renovate GitHub App is already installed
Renovate will necessarily reduce PR count
permission to bypass interactive GitHub App authorization
permission to override foreign leases or dirty worktrees
metadata.known_catalog_gap: repo.mitschreiber is not currently registered as a Bureau repository resource
metadata.registered_from: User directive 2026-07-23 after system-wide Renovate architecture review and live comparison against existing Fleet, Bureau and Grabowski contracts.

supersedes_prior_decision.scope: This task supersedes the no-go only for designing and implementing a staged Fleet-bound replacement foundation; repository activation remains separately gated.

rollback.strategy: Revert the metarepo Renovate V1 policy, preset and scope-validation changes. Because this task performs no consumer-repository cutover and grants no GitHub App permissions, rollback does not require restoring Dependabot configs or changing external bot state.

title: Establish Fleet-bound Renovate V1 policy and rollout contract
